channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Immutable backup storage is the best protection against ransomware, but many businesses don’t have it
item.description: Immutable backup storage is key to protect against ransomware attacks, but many businesses don't use it.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy predicted that the retailer’s web of third-party sellers will try to pass the extra costs incurred by the Trump administration's wide-ranging tariffs on to buyers.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti benchmark leak hints at performance boost over its predecessor – but it won’t matter if it doesn’t stay at retail price
item.description: A new benchmark leak suggests Nvidia's RTX 5060 Ti may be great for budget GPU gamers, but that may not matter due to the state of GPU prices.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WhatsApp users fume over new Meta AI button that you can’t remove – here’s what it does
item.description: WhatsApp has rolled out its controversial Meta AI chatbot update in more countries – and many users aren't happy.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are demanding President Trump confirm that Elon Musk will leave his role in the administration by May 30. A group of 77 lawmakers, led by Rep.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google reaffirms $75B AI infra investment as cloud providers pursue divergent strategies
item.description: In a move closely watched by enterprise technology leaders, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai has reaffirmed Google’s commitment to spending $75 billion this year on AI infrastructure and data centers — weeks after Microsoft reportedly abandoned many of its data center projects.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Is Microsoft getting truly desperate with adverts now? I’m seriously unimpressed with a new ‘suggestion’ to buy Avowed in Windows 10 and Windows 11
item.description: Avowed might be good, but I don’t want Windows 10 shoving adverts in my face urging me to buy the game, thanks Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drones are the future of cybercrime
item.description: The Ukrainian military is teaching the world how to creatively use drones for battlefield offense and defense. Ukraine has built a vast arsenal of drones customized for precision strikes, reconnaissance, and kamikaze missions. But this innovation also extends to cybersecurity offense and defense.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump's China tariffs derail push for TikTok deal
item.description: President Trump’s new tariffs on China have thrown a wrench in efforts to negotiate a deal over TikTok’s divestment from its parent company ByteDance, as Washington and Beijing sink deeper into a trade war.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: 10 highest-paying IT skills in 2025 so far
item.description: IT has always been known as a lucrative industry for job seekers, with tech professionals earning an average annual salary of $112,521, according to data from the 2025 Dice Tech Salary Report.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft shelves another $1bn data center project as tariff fears rise
item.description: Worried about the impacts of global trade tariffs, Microsoft has paused plans to build three US data center campuses.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Adding smarts to Azure data lakes with Fabric Data Agents
item.description: Enterprise AI needs one thing if it’s to get around the limitations of large language models and deliver the results businesses need from their agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: April update has broken Windows Hello for some devices but there’s a quick fix
item.description: A software hiccup has occurred with Windows Hello following this month’s security updates, one that could prevent users from logging in via facial recognition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IETF hatching a new way to tame aggressive AI website scraping
item.description: For web publishers, stopping AI bots from scraping their best content while consuming valuable bandwidth must feel somewhere between futile and nigh impossible. It’s like throwing a cup of water at a forest fire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Former Facebook executive tells Senate committee company undermined US national security with China
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, a former executive at Meta and author of an explosive insider account of her time at the social media giant, testified before the Senate Judiciary Committee Wednesday, accusing the company of undermining national security and briefing China on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft says it's “slowing or pausing” some data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: Travelers should prepare for extra scrutiny of devices when crossing borders, especially when entering the U.S. Experts say 100% privacy might be impossible but there things you can do to make it harder for someone to see your private data.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ignoring Wall Street, Apple gets busy on Vision Pro 2.0
item.description: While politicians everywhere seem to be doing their best to make everything pretty difficult, the North Star for Apple remains what it always has been: creating products people seem to love. That’s what got the company through (and out of) tough times before.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UALink releases inaugural GPU interconnect specification
item.description: The multivendor Ultra Accelerator Link (UALink) consortium has published its first specification aimed at delivering an open standard interconnect for AI clusters – and providing an alternative to Nvidia’s proprietary NVLink.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta helped build China’s DeepSeek: Whistleblower testimony
item.description: Former Meta executive Sarah Wynn-Williams is set to testify before the US Senate Judiciary Committee on Wednesday, revealing how the company’s AI model, Llama, played a critical role in accelerating China’s AI capabilities — particularly contributing to the rise of DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Google to add on-demand genAI data analyst to Workspace
item.description: Google’s new on-demand generative AI (genAI) analyst for spreadsheets stood out among a slew of new AI features for its Workplace productivity suite announced at this week’s Cloud Next event in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Humanoid robot breakdances its way into history
item.description: Atlas robot by Boston Dynamics amazes tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson with its breakdancing moves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is Kubernetes? Scalable cloud-native applications
item.description: Kubernetes is a popular open source platform for container orchestration—that is, for managing applications built from multiple, largely self-contained runtimes called containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Man who used AI lawyer explains why he did so after judge got angry
item.description: Dewald maintains he received prior approval for his presentation method, though not specifically for an AI-generated speaker.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Musk’s brother urges Trump to fire Navarro
item.description: Elon Musk’s brother, Kimball Musk, urged President Trump to fire his senior trade advisor Peter Navarro on Tuesday night. “@realDonaldTrump Mr President, if Peter Navarro has lied to you about Ron Vara, what else has he lied to you about?” Kimball Musk said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: From training dogs to intelligent machines: Here’s how reinforcement learning is teaching AI
item.description: Understanding intelligence and creating intelligent machines are grand scientific challenges of our times. The ability to learn from experience is a cornerstone of intelligence for machines and living beings alike.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Safeguarding Critical Infrastructure: Stronger Governance Alone Isn't Enough
item.description: The Transportation Security Administration's proposed rulemaking on cyber risk management and reporting requirements for surface transportation owners and operators is a necessary evolution in critical infrastructure protection.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Beware these 10 malicious VS Code extensions
item.description: Developers using Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code (VSCode) editor are being warned to delete, or at least stay away from, 10 newly published extensions which will trigger the installation of a cryptominer.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Android feature prevents sensitive content in Google Messages
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how Google kept Android SafetyCore details quiet until it scanned users' photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
